checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.location: 10-Q comparative balance sheet, FY2012 revenue from 10-K

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill=4754333721.11 (2012-09-30) vs IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill=7572499467.46 (2012-12-31) — ~59% one-quarter increase
Revenues=4725558185.57 (Q3 2012) vs Revenues=4354454816.41 (Q4 2012) — revenue was flat-to-down over the same quarter that Goodwill and Intangibles surged

Assets=36695484290.37 (2012-09-30) jumping to Assets=43732353512.90 (2012-12-31), with DebtAndCapitalLeaseObligations rising from 23890545635.19 (2012-09-30) to 29013297108.97 (2012-12-31), consistent with a debt-financed acquisition (tender offer activity noted in filing chronology, e.g. SC TO-T filed 2012-09-10 through 2012-11-20)

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=-2266333533.98 (FY2008) vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=4574354221.23 (FY2008)
evidence.location: 10-K FY2010, prior-year comparative cash flow/income statement

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=-236676056.62 (FY2009) vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=3180134263.35 (FY2009)
NetIncomeLoss=-90229201.76 (FY2010) vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=4148017299.00 (FY2010)
NetIncomeLossAvailableToCommonStockholdersBasic=330856682.92 (FY2011) vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivitiesContinuingOperations=4194330098.01 (FY2011)
ProfitLoss=456515363.72 (FY2012) vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivitiesContinuingOperations=5104517452.46 (FY2012)

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: InventoryNet=175438366.55 (2009-12-31) growing to InventoryNet=240954262.46 (2013-06-30), roughly in line with revenue growth over the same span, but no CostOfRevenue/GrossProfit line items were provided to test margin trends
evidence.location: Balance sheet series; no income-statement COGS/gross-margin concept supplied in dataset

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=-282468634.81 (Q1 2010), NetIncomeLoss=-47178581.66 (Q2 2010), NetIncomeLoss=294178298.30 (Q3 2010) — large swings quarter to quarter
NetIncomeLossAvailableToCommonStockholdersBasic=-105734411.35 (Q1 2012) vs 456179192.14 (Q3 2012) — earnings swing from loss to sizable profit within the same year

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: Liabilities=26114292428.29 (2009-12-31) growing to Liabilities=39023510003.37 (2012-12-31) and Liabilities=44637763099.28 (2013-06-30), tracking or exceeding asset/revenue growth
No specific allowance for doubtful accounts, warranty reserve, or similar contra-asset/reserve concept was provided in the dataset, limiting assessment of reserve adequacy specifically

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: NT 10-K filed 2009-03-03 (same day as the 10-K itself, filed 2009-03-03)
10-K/A filed 2010-03-01, shortly after the original 10-K filed 2010-02-26
NT 10-K filed 2013-03-04, the same date as the 10-K itself (filed 2013-03-04)

Heavy cluster of SC TO-T / SC TO-T/A tender-offer filings from 2012-09-10 through 2012-11-20, coincident with the Q4 2012 jump in Goodwill/Intangibles/Debt, indicating a material acquisition event around that time

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: NetCashProvidedByUsedInFinancingActivities and NetCashProvidedByUsedInInvestingActivities series end at Q1 2011 (last point: -1674834953.89 for financing, -570824967.30 for investing, both period 2011-01-01 to 2011-03-31), leaving no coverage for FY2011-FY2013 financing/investing cash flow trends

No CostOfRevenue/GrossProfit concept was provided at any point in the series, preventing assessment of gross margin trend (CL4) despite Revenues and SellingGeneralAndAdministrativeExpense being available

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Purchase-accounting allocation from a large debt-financed acquisition completed around Q4 2012 (evidenced by tender-offer filings Sept-Nov 2012) could result in aggressive fair-value allocations to goodwill/intangibles versus tangible/depreciable assets, potentially deferring expense recognition and inflating non-current soft assets relative to the acquired business's revenue contribution.

top_signals: Goodwill and Intangibles roughly tripled and grew 59% respectively in a single quarter (Q4 2012), far outpacing flat/declining quarterly revenue, coincident with heavy tender-offer (M&A) filing activity

Persistent, large multi-year gap between reported Net Income/ProfitLoss and Operating Cash Flow (e.g., FY2008 net loss of -2.27B vs +4.57B operating cash flow; FY2012 net income of 0.46B vs 5.10B operating cash flow)
Two 'NT 10-K' late-filing notifications (2009 and 2013) filed on the same date as the corresponding 10-K, plus a 10-K/A amendment in 2010, indicating recurring filing-timeliness/restatement friction
Material data gaps (no cost-of-revenue/gross-margin line items; financing/investing cash flow data stops at Q1 2011) limit full verification of margin and cash-flow-statement consistency
